title: National Bank of Kuwait - Egypt (NBK) (Egypt) Profile & Reviews
description: Explore a detailed profile and institutional data for National Bank of Kuwait - Egypt (NBK) in Egypt. Share your experience or access institutional-grade data for professional insights.

faq.question: What is National Bank of Kuwait - Egypt (NBK)?
faq.answer: National Bank of Kuwait - Egypt (NBK) is a leading commercial bank operating in Egypt as a subsidiary of the National Bank of Kuwait, one of the largest and most highly-rated financial institutions in the Middle East. NBK-Egypt serves both retail and corporate customers, offering a comprehensive range of banking and financial services. The bank operates approximately 53 branches across strategic locations throughout Egypt and has established itself as a prominent player in the Egyptian banking sector, particularly recognized for its expertise in serving small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs).

faq.question: When was National Bank of Kuwait - Egypt (NBK) founded?
faq.answer: The bank's history dates back to May 1, 1980, when it was originally established as Al Watany Bank of Egypt by several prominent Egyptian businessmen under Law No. 93 of 1974. The bank began operations with just two branches in Cairo. In August 2007, during a period of substantial Egyptian economic growth, the National Bank of Kuwait acquired a 51% stake in Al Watany Bank of Egypt for US $516 million. By October 2007, NBK had increased its ownership to 93.77% for a total investment exceeding US $900 million. This acquisition marked NBK's first entry into the Egyptian market and represented a significant milestone in the bank's regional expansion strategy. Following the acquisition, the bank initially operated as Al Watany Bank of Egypt with the tagline "a subsidiary of NBK Group." In 2014, it was officially rebranded as National Bank of Kuwait - Egypt.

faq.question: Where is National Bank of Kuwait - Egypt (NBK) headquartered?
faq.answer: National Bank of Kuwait - Egypt is headquartered in Cairo, Egypt. The bank's central operations are based in the Egyptian capital, which serves as the hub for its nationwide banking activities and strategic management.

faq.question: Where does National Bank of Kuwait - Egypt (NBK) operate?
faq.answer: NBK-Egypt operates across multiple governorates and cities throughout Egypt. The bank has expanded from its original 24 branches at the time of acquisition to approximately 53 branches as of 2022. These branches are strategically located in vital areas including Cairo, Giza, Alexandria, Mansoura, Damietta, Port Said, Assiut, and Sohag. The bank's nationwide presence ensures accessibility for both individual and corporate customers across Egypt's major economic centers and growing regions.

faq.question: What services does National Bank of Kuwait - Egypt (NBK) offer?
faq.answer: NBK-Egypt provides a full spectrum of financial services organized across three main divisions: Retail Banking: Current and savings accounts with competitive interest rates, personal loans with flexible repayment terms, diverse credit card offerings with rewards programs and travel benefits, mortgage solutions for home ownership, and certificates of deposit. Business Banking: Customized lending products for working capital and expansion projects, comprehensive trade finance facilities including letters of credit and guarantees, cash management services, and specialized solutions for small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs). Treasury and Investment Services: Foreign exchange services at competitive rates, investment advisory and portfolio management, fixed-income products, and risk management solutions. Digital Banking Services: The bank offers comprehensive online banking through its user-friendly website and mobile banking application. Customers can manage accounts, transfer funds locally and internationally, pay bills, open new accounts, apply for loans and cards, and monitor transactions. NBK-Egypt has made significant investments in digital infrastructure, incorporating enhanced security features including two-factor authentication and encryption.

faq.question: What makes National Bank of Kuwait - Egypt (NBK) unique?
faq.answer: NBK-Egypt distinguishes itself through several key characteristics. The bank leverages the resources, expertise, and international standing of its parent company, the National Bank of Kuwait, which is consistently rated among the top banks in the Middle East by international rating agencies. The bank has built its reputation on the tagline "The Bank You Know And Trust," reflecting its stable management, consistent profitability, high asset quality, and strong capitalization. NBK-Egypt has demonstrated exceptional financial performance, reporting EGP 4.021 billion in net profit for FY2023, representing significant year-over-year growth. Since its acquisition in 2007, the bank has pursued an aggressive expansion strategy, increasing its paid-in capital to five billion Egyptian Pounds in 2021 pursuant to Egyptian banking regulations. The bank particularly excels in serving the SME market segment, maintaining its historical reputation as a leader in this niche while also developing comprehensive services for retail and institutional clients.

faq.question: What type of financial institution is National Bank of Kuwait - Egypt (NBK)?
faq.answer: National Bank of Kuwait - Egypt is a universal commercial bank operating under Egyptian banking regulations. It functions as a full-service financial institution providing both retail and corporate banking services. As a commercial bank in Egypt, NBK-Egypt is regulated by the Central Bank of Egypt and operates in accordance with the Egyptian Banking Law. The bank combines traditional commercial banking activities with modern investment services, positioning itself as a comprehensive financial services provider. Its structure as a subsidiary of an internationally recognized banking group provides additional strength and credibility while maintaining its focus on the Egyptian market's specific needs and opportunities.

faq.question: How has National Bank of Kuwait - Egypt (NBK) performed financially?
faq.answer: NBK-Egypt has demonstrated strong and consistent financial performance since its acquisition by the National Bank of Kuwait. The bank reported a remarkable 157% increase in net profit in 2007 compared to 2006, achieving 243.6 million Egyptian pounds. This growth trajectory has continued, with the bank posting EGP 4.021 billion (equivalent to KWD 41.4 million) in net profit for FY2023. By the first half of FY2025, total assets reached approximately EGP 206 billion, while the loan portfolio grew by around 10%. Net interest income, the bank's primary revenue driver, has shown particularly strong growth, reaching EGP 11.6 billion with a 66% increase year-over-year. Total revenue (Net Operating Income) reached EGP 13.5 billion, marking a 57% increase. The bank's efficiency metrics have also improved, with the cost-to-net operating income ratio decreasing from 26% to approximately 24%, demonstrating enhanced operational effectiveness and expense control.
